D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Lloyd's Single-Mirror Interference

interfere a source with its own mirror image and find the tell-tale dark edge fringe

A source and its mirror image form two coherent beams separated by 2h. Because reflection flips the phase by π, the fringes are the inverse of Young's — dark where the path difference is zero.

Raise the source height h above the mirror, set the wavelength, and slide the screen distance L. Watch the virtual source S′ and the dark fringe pinned to the mirror edge.
